---
title: "Simulation Fidelity and Physical Constraints"
chapter_id: "simulation-fidelity-and-physical-constraints"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "simulation_scaling"
  - "cgs"
  - "rankfold_neuralfold"
  - "tokenmana"
  - "alignment_field"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Simulation Fidelity and Physical Constraints

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `simulation-fidelity-and-physical-constraints` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `simulation_scaling`, `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold`, `tokenmana`, `alignment_field` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The architecture needs a realism check on simulations, nested worlds, fidelity, clockspeed, and compute constraints.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Simulation-based claims can become unbounded if scope, fidelity, resource cost, and observer effects are not explicit.

## Core claim

[simulation-fidelity-and-physical-constraints.core, support: argument] Simulation and fidelity claims should be bounded by resource constraints and used as design constraints rather than assumed unlimited substrates.

## Mechanism

- Define scope, clockspeed, fidelity, and resource demand.
- Use simulation limits to constrain roadmap and benchmark claims.
- Record liberties or approximations explicitly.

## Interfaces

- Efficiency theory uses resource constraints.
- Benchmarks record fidelity limits.
- Alignment scenarios avoid unbounded simulation claims.

## Invariants

- Fidelity is declared.
- Resource bounds are explicit.
- Speculative simulation metaphysics stays separated from engineering claims.

## Failure modes

- Overclaiming nested simulation feasibility.
- Ignoring resource ceilings.
- Treating approximate simulations as ground truth.

## Minimal implementation

A simulation-fidelity checklist and cost/fidelity table for experiment design.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Fidelity declaration test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Resource-bound sanity check | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Simulation approximation audit | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `simulation_scaling` | Simulation Scaling Law | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Resource constraints on scope, clockspeed, and fidelity in simulations. |
| `cgs` | Compact Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Smallest adequate structure that can generate/govern target without hiding residual complexity. |
| `rankfold_neuralfold` | RankFold + NeuralFold | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Tensor/artifact compression. Low-rank residual coding plus functional preprocessing and probe-route fallback. |
| `tokenmana` | TokenMana | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Regenerative capacity mechanisms for load-stable token pricing. |
| `alignment_field` | Field of God / Alignment Field family | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Primary Alignment Field draft found. Use with Ethica Mechanica and Eternal Code. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
